title: Support Salesforce Private Reports Visibility
story: As a Salesforce administrator, I want to view private reports in DataTools Pro, so that I can audit personal reports for important content that needs to be supported or migrated.
description:
Enable visibility of Salesforce personal/private reports in the Reports & Dashboards page. This is planned as a premium feature.

### Decision
The `USING SCOPE allPrivate` approach was rejected because it bypasses the DataTools user invitation model. Instead, reports with a 'Private' folder name from the standard SOQL query are identified as private.

### Tiers
- **Free version**: Current user sees only their own personal reports
- **Premium feature**: View all private reports across the organization (future upsell)

### Navigation
Reports & Dashboards page will have three sections:
- Salesforce Reports
- Salesforce Dashboards
- Salesforce Personal Reports

### Technical Approach
Private reports are identified by checking if the `FolderName` returned from the standard Report SOQL query is 'Private'. This avoids a separate `USING SCOPE allPrivate` query.
acceptance_criteria: Private reports are identified by FolderName = 'Private' in the standard SOQL query
Personal Reports section appears as a separate category in Reports & Dashboards
Free tier shows only the current user's personal reports
Private reports are stored and displayed separately from public reports
ux_changes: New 'Salesforce Personal Reports' section on the Reports & Dashboards page.
middleware_changes: Logic to identify private reports by FolderName and separate them from public reports.
data_model_changes: Reports marked with a private flag based on FolderName = 'Private'.
api_changes: Extended report fetching SOQL to capture FolderName for private report identification.
implementation_notes: The USING SCOPE allPrivate SOQL approach was rejected in favor of checking FolderName = 'Private'. Developer confirmed private reports are already captured by the existing query and just need to be flagged/separated in the UI. This is designed as a premium feature for future monetization.
comment_summary: Discussion about whether to use USING SCOPE allPrivate or FolderName-based detection. Developer confirmed private reports are already in the database via the standard query. The allPrivate scope was rejected for bypassing user invitation. Developer fetched and stored private reports, awaiting frontend integration decision.
